div.head: The Chargé in Siam (Yost) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 353. Siamese Mission headed by brother former Prime Minister KhuangPridi Phanomyong succeeded Khuang Aphaiwong as Prime Minister on March 25, 1946. left for Saigon today together with D’ArgenlieuAdm. Thierry d’Argenlieu, French High Commissioner for Indochina. and French Minister of ColoniesMarius Moutet. now in Saigon. Mission exploratory in character as previously outlined to me by Foreign Minister.Direk Chainam, also known as Direck Jayanam. (Reference paragraph 5 mytel 285, March 26Not printed; this paragraph stated: “Direk said he is recommending to Cabinet that French negotiations be inaugurated by despatch private mission to Saigon rather than immediate formal negotiations Singapore. He feels it better ground be first explored informally rather than risking impasse in formal negotiations. Upon being asked my opinion I said that modus did not seem to me important as long as action was rapid and clear cut along lines previously recommended by US and British Governments. Direk indicated he felt Siam had no alternative but to accept these recommendations.” (892.00/3–2646)). I repeated to Foreign Minister at that interview US views on restoration Indochina territories. (ReDeptel 202 March 29Not printed, but see footnote 25, p. 986.) Siamese seem reconciled to restoration but still hope for later “generous gesture” on part of French. Our views thereon set forth mytel 202, March 9 [29]. Foreign Minister has promised to keep us fully informed course negotiations.
